title: Vinted Alerts Without the Ban Risk
intro: Vinted alert tools fall into two categories: alert-only extensions that notify you when a listing matches your search, and autocop bots that log into your account and complete purchases automatically. The second type carries real account-suspension risk because it mimics automated login behavior that Vinted's fraud detection is built to flag.
meta_description: Why alert-only tools like Supervint are safer than autocop bots for your Vinted account — and what actually triggers a ban.
author: Supervint Team

body.heading: What actually triggers a Vinted ban
paragraphs: Vinted's systems watch for behavior that doesn't look human: logging in at unusual hours, taking actions in rapid succession, or repeating the same action pattern hundreds of times a day. Autocop and auto-buy bots need to log into your account directly to complete a purchase on your behalf — that login-and-act pattern is exactly what automated fraud detection is built to catch.

An alert-only extension never logs into your account or takes actions on your behalf. It watches public search results and notifies you — the actual purchase still happens through your normal, manual browser session, the same way it would if you'd refreshed the page yourself.

body.heading: Alert-only vs. auto-buy: the practical difference
paragraphs: Auto-buy tools promise speed — the bot completes checkout in the moment a listing appears, before a human could react. That speed is also the risk: it requires standing account access and rapid automated actions, which is the exact pattern platforms design their bot-detection around.

Alert-only tools trade a few seconds of speed for account safety. You still get notified the moment a match appears — you just complete the purchase yourself, which keeps every action on your account genuinely human.

body.heading: How to tell which type of tool you're using
paragraphs: Check whether the tool asks for your Vinted login credentials during setup. If it does, and it completes purchases without you clicking anything, it's an auto-buy tool. If it only asks you to define a search and sends a notification you then act on yourself, it's alert-only.

faq.question: Can using a Vinted alert extension get my account banned?
faq.answer: An alert-only extension that doesn't log into your account or take automated actions carries minimal ban risk, since every purchase still happens through your normal manual session.

faq.question: What's the difference between an alert tool and an autocop bot?
faq.answer: An alert tool notifies you when a listing matches your search; an autocop bot logs into your account and completes the purchase automatically, which requires standing account access.

faq.question: Is it against Vinted's terms to use an alert extension?
faq.answer: Alert-only tools that simply monitor public search results without accessing your account or automating actions operate differently from bots that log in and act on your behalf — check Vinted's current terms of service for specifics.
